focus: current model evidence, constraints, clearance, and manufacturing gaps

risks.name: drive_unit-1
risks.why: floating components may indicate unconstrained assembly intent.

risks.name: support_bushing-1
risks.why: suppressed components can hide missing geometry, mates, or interference.

risks.name: reference_sensor-1
risks.why: floating components may indicate unconstrained assembly intent.

risks.name: MateGroup
risks.why: mate state is often decisive for assembly intent, spatial freedom, and downstream edits.

evidence_gaps.why: No component bounding boxes are present; spatial adjacency, stack order, and clearance reasoning will be weak.
No obvious hole/manufacturing features were reported; hole series, access, and process assumptions need targeted inspection.
Floating components exist; confirm whether this is intended motion, incomplete mating, or stale assembly state.

next_queries.why: Build a task-aware spatial evidence model before deciding what to edit.
Pull only the objects relevant to the current question instead of flooding the model with unrelated report data.
Summarize verifiable risks, open questions, and candidate actions without forcing a fixed workflow.
Plan a reversible one-variable-at-a-time edit only after the evidence is sufficient.

recommended_commands: swctl.ps1 rebuild; swctl.ps1 interference; swctl.ps1 compare after each narrow change
handoff_note: Do not blindly replay templates or force a fixed output shape: use live evidence, targeted queries, reversible edits, and verification so the model can reason flexibly from the actual CAD state.
